headline: Transcript: Wall Street’s ‘sigh of relief’ over Trump’s Treasury pick
summary: Kasia Broussalian talks to Colby Smith and Michael Stott
body_text:
This is an audio transcript of the FT News Briefing podcast episode: ‘Wall Street’s “sigh of relief” over Trump’s Treasury pick’

Kasia Broussalian Good morning from the Financial Times. Today is Monday, November 25th. And this is your FT News Briefing.

Huawei is getting ready to launch a smartphone with all-Chinese software. And the world has just barely come together to support climate finance. Plus, we look at how China is winning friends and influencing people in Latin America.

Michael Stott Its companies have been buying up strategic stakes and investing in strategic projects across the region.

Kasia Broussalian I’m Kasia Broussalian, and here’s the news you need to start your day. [MUSIC PLAYING]Huawei has a new operating system and it’s turning heads. We’ll get a proper look on Tuesday when the Chinese tech giant launches its latest flagship phone. The device will run on something called HarmonyOS Next, and will have all its own native apps. That’s a major development for Hua
